other_observers: Christopher L. Wood; I found and identified the bird
specific_location: N side of 3V Road about 1 mile E of Hwy 71
description: The bird was a large Empidonax flycatcher that was obviously larger than a nearby Least Flycatcher. The crown, nape, back, and rump were all the same deep green that was brighter than olive. The bird's bill was comprised of a black maxilla and an orangish mandible and was fairly long and wide. The dark eyes were nearly completely surrounded by very thin yellowish eye rings, broken only slightly on the top. The auriculars contrasted strongly and quite sharply with the white chin and throat.  The wings were blackish, with strong white wing bars that had a slight yellow cast and white-fringed tertials. The primary projection was quite long for an Empidonax, at least equalling 1/3 of the length of the longest tertial. The tail was dark with a strong olive cast and the outer rectrices sported whitish outer webs. The belly, sides, flanks, and vent were pale and washed variably with pale yellow. The legs were dark.Behaviors: Foraging low in Russian OlivesCall: None heardPlumage: Alternate
